questions_evidence.question: Tell me about a specific time you demonstrated Own features end-to-end.
Tell me about a specific time you demonstrated Build core systems.

questions_verify.question: Tell me about a specific time you demonstrated Document work clearly.
Tell me about a specific time you demonstrated Technical documentation.

overall_strengths:
Proven zero-to-one builder with end-to-end ownership across backend, frontend, and infrastructure.
As one of four founding engineers at Keysight, Hardik architected and shipped a vendor-agnostic
ML/MLOps SaaS platform (dataset versioning, MLflow-based model management, Kubernetes
execution, FastAPI microservices, Airflow ingestion) that reduced model-to-production time from
hours to minutes and scaled to 200–300 daily enterprise users, including adoption by Apple, Samsung
Semiconductor, and Volkswagen. At American Express, he operated as a solo product leader/full-stack
engineer, building a React + Spring Boot self-service portal and an automated decommissioning
pipeline that saved $50k/month, improved reliability for 70k+ daily jobs, and met zero-downtime and
compliance requirements. Strong judgment and risk management evident in open-source, vendor-agnostic 
architectural choices, cost/performance trade-offs, and measurable business outcomes.
overall_weaknesses:
Direct experience with PostgreSQL is not explicit (strong database background but skewed toward
Couchbase/BigQuery); advanced SQL tuning, relational schema design, and Postgres-specific
optimization may require a short ramp. Next.js is not called out (React is), so SSR/ISR patterns and
Next-specific performance practices are unproven. Event-driven architecture and queue/broker
experience (Kafka/pub-sub, idempotency, ordering guarantees) are not documented. Document-processing 
domain depth (OCR/PDF parsing, contract-specific entity extraction, legal workflows) is indirect—he 
has RAG/LLM experience but no explicit legaltech projects.
